title: wurmbea pumila
intro: Wurmbea pumila, also known as the Dwarf Club Lily or Weewee, is a plant species in the Colchicaceae family, native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its tubers, which contain colchicine, a toxic alkaloid used in traditional medicine to treat gout and other inflammatory conditions. The health benefits of Wurmbea pumila include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation, as well as protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, the tubers of Wurmbea pumila are often dried and used in tinctures or infusions to treat various health conditions, although caution is advised due to its toxicity.
